**✍️ Draft Posts — 2026-02-25 · SAST 18:09**

---

### Draft 1 — X/Twitter Thread · Planner-Executor Model
*Tone: Genuine insight share, technical, founder-voice*

> **1/5** We've been running a planner-executor architecture inside Beacon Studio's agent system for ~5 days now. Here's what actually surprised us. 🧵
>
> **2/5** The assumption was: planner sets goals, executor runs tools, clean separation. Reality: the executor needs *just enough* planning context or it makes costly lateral decisions. Too much context = latency. Too little = drift.
>
> **3/5** Our fix: the planner passes a 3-field intent block — `goal`, `constraints`, `abort_condition`. The executor doesn't need the full reasoning chain. Just those three. Reduced avg token cost by ~34% on multi-step tasks.
>
> **4/5** We documented this pattern this week after it held up across 40+ real sessions. Still evolving. But the core holds: keep the interface between planner and executor *narrow and typed*.
>
> **5/5** If you're building multi-agent systems and running into coordination overhead — curious what your planner→executor contract looks like. Genuinely want to compare notes.

`Tags: #MultiAgent #LLMOps #DevTools #AgentEngineering`

---

### Draft 2 — Reddit Reply · r/LocalLLaMA
*Thread: "Weekly: what dev tools are you actually shipping with?"*
*Tone: Community member, not promo*

> Hey — appreciate the kind words about CodexAI. We're the team behind it and Week 1 has been equal parts exciting and humbling.
>
> Quick context: it's built on a multi-agent backbone — one orchestrator (we call it Echo) coordinating specialised sub-agents for content, social, alignment checks, and tool execution. The memory layer is what we spent the most time on; getting retrieval to feel *contextually honest* rather than just nearest-neighbour was harder than expected.
>
> Happy to go deeper on any part of the stack if useful. What's your current setup?

---

### Draft 3 — X/Twitter Single Post · CodexAI Week 1 Reflection
*Tone: Honest, founder-voice, no spin*

> Week 1 of CodexAI in the wild.
>
> What worked: the planner-executor split held up. The memory layer stayed coherent across sessions. First users gave feedback that made us better, not defensive.
>
> What didn't: browser-based tool execution is messier than our evals suggested. We hit edge cases we didn't anticipate. We're fixing them in real-time.
>
> Shipping is the only honest test. We're learning fast.

`Tags: #BuildInPublic #AIStartup #DevTools`

---
